Hero.blurb: molave.ai is one product with three features — an AI interviewer, a meeting assistant, and quiet room awareness — built entirely on AWS by people who’d rather be listening.
Hero.quote: “The most important conversations are also the most broken. We’re trying to mend a few of them.”
Hero.venueTag: Built entirely on AWS · Phase 1 lands June 2026

Manifesto.blurb: Meetings and interviews are where modern work is decided. They are also where attention thins, voices go missing, and good people are reduced to surface impressions. The technology in the room is designed to record — not to help people pay attention to each other.
Manifesto.rowMeetingsBody: Hosts cannot track who is engaged across a screen full of faces. Decisions and action items disappear. Quiet contributors stay quiet. Anyone who missed the meeting must chase what happened.
Manifesto.rowInterviewsBody: Candidates are judged on surface presence rather than substance. Recruiters miss strong candidates because they’re exhausted by the volume. Evaluation reports fixate on impression.
Manifesto.rowBothBody: Time is consumed without producing outcomes. The room remembers nothing it wasn’t told to.

Features.sectionBlurb: The same room hosts your daily syncs and your interviews. Three features run inside it — together, never in isolation — and every one of them is built so the people in the room can pay better attention to each other.

interviewer.name: AI Interviewer
interviewer.blurb: Runs the interview alone or sits beside a human as a quiet co-pilot. Listens patiently, follows up on substance, and treats every candidate with the same dignity — regardless of fluency, accent, or composure.
interviewer.bullet1: Configurable per role: questions, rubric, depth
interviewer.bullet2: Voice-only or video persona — your choice

meetingAssistant.name: AI Meeting Assistant
meetingAssistant.blurb: Captures decisions, tracks action items, and helps the quieter voices be heard. Sends personalized summaries afterward so anyone who missed it can catch up — even the people who weren’t in the call.
meetingAssistant.bullet2: Live agenda position for the host
meetingAssistant.bullet4: Pre-meeting check: should this be a meeting at all?

roomAwareness.name: Room Awareness
roomAwareness.blurb: Reads facial geometry, voice cadence, and transcript context — and offers gentle private suggestions to the host. Never categorical labels. Never broadcast. Always the kinder reading.
roomAwareness.bullet1: Multi-signal fusion — never one signal alone
roomAwareness.bullet4: Bias-audited across skin tones, ages, cultures

Safeguards.blurb: Seven hard rules, particularly around Room Awareness. Each one is a guarantee, not a guideline. Together they’re the difference between attention sensing and surveillance.
Safeguards.rule01Body: A suggestion fires only when multiple signals agree. Facial geometry alone is not enough. Voice prosody alone is not enough. Transcript alone is not enough.
Safeguards.rule02Body: Below a defined threshold, the system says nothing. Silence is preferred over a wrong suggestion.
Safeguards.rule03Body: When signals are ambiguous, the system defaults to the kinder interpretation. Thinking, not disengaged. Considering, not confused.
Safeguards.rule04Body: Every nudge is dismissible. Nothing is broadcast. No long-term profile of any person is retained.
Safeguards.rule05Body: Interpretation is tested across skin tones, ages, and cultures before any rollout.
Safeguards.rule06Body: An optional view shows what signals the AI is currently picking up. Hosts can see why the system is behaving as it is.
Safeguards.rule07Body: All Room Awareness signal processing follows the privacy commitments described in that section.

Scenario.blurb: A hiring manager begins a first-round interview. Watch what the room does without anyone asking it to.
Scenario.beat1Body: Greets the candidate warmly in her preferred language, explains the format, and asks the first question — tailored to the role.
Scenario.beat2Body: She hesitates mid-answer. Her face shows that she is composing a thought, not finishing one.
Scenario.beat3Body: Three signals agree — pause length, mouth state, eye direction. A private nudge is sent to the hiring manager: “She has more to say. Give her a few seconds.”
Scenario.beat4Body: Waits. The candidate completes her answer. The room is quiet on purpose.
Scenario.beat5Body: The manager receives a substance-focused evaluation. The candidate receives a respectful summary of what she communicated well, plus her full transcript and recording.

Cta.blurb: Private preview for selected Persol teams through Q3 2026. If you’d like to interview, meet, or just listen with us — we’d love to hear from you.
Cta.alreadyMemberSuffix: if you already have a workspace.

Footer.tagline: An intelligence layer for meetings and interviews — built entirely on AWS by the Persol Frontier AI Lab.
Footer.credits: Persol Frontier · AWS · prepared by John & Carrie

Auth.loginShellBlurb: A quieter interface for hiring conversations — built on the rhythms of real work, not the scripts of legacy ATS.
Auth.loginShellCaption: “The room remembers what the rubric forgets.”
Auth.loginSubhead: Sign in to resume your sessions, transcripts, and saved rubrics.
Auth.registerShellBlurb: Set up a workspace for your team. Bring your rubrics, your taste, and the questions you’ve been refining for years.
Auth.registerShellCaption: “Every careful interview is a small act of editing.”
Auth.registerSubhead: Free during the private preview — no card, no commitment.

Dashboard.greetingBlurb: A quiet stretch ahead — three rooms on the calendar and two evaluations waiting for your read. Room Awareness is calibrated for today’s sessions.
Dashboard.nowMeta: Jamie Rivera · 45 minutes · AI Interviewer w/ co-pilot
Dashboard.awarenessBlurb: Private nudges from Room Awareness — never broadcast, always dismissible.
Dashboard.closingQuote: “The room remembers what the rubric forgets.”
Dashboard.closingBlurb: Calibrate Room Awareness, review the persona settings, or step into your next room.
